# servicenow_oauth_entity (Resource)

`servicenow_oauth_entity` manages an OAuth application entity within ServiceNow.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Manages an OAuth application entity in ServiceNow.
resource "servicenow_oauth_entity" "example" {
  name                   = "example-oauth-entity"
  redirect_url           = "https://example.com/callback"
  access_token_lifespan  = 1800
  refresh_token_lifespan = 8640000
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Name of the OAuth app.

### Optional

- `access` (String) Whether this Script can be accessed from only this application scope or all application scopes. Values can be 'package_private' or 'public'.
- `access_token_lifespan` (Number) Number of seconds a newly created access token is good for.
- `logo_url` (String) URL of the logo image to display for the OAuth application.
- `redirect_url` (String) The OAuth app's endpoint to receive the authorization code.
- `refresh_token_lifespan` (Number) Number of seconds the refresh token is good for.
- `scope` (String) Associates a resource to a specific application ID in ServiceNow.

### Read-Only

- `client_id` (String, Sensitive) OAuth Client ID required during handshake.
- `client_uuid` (String) Internal unique identifier of the entity.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import using the sys_id of the record in ServiceNow.
terraform import servicenow_oauth_entity.example <sys_id>
```
